items.title: Thousands gather in Minneapolis for anti-ICE protest
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of people marched in Minneapolis Saturday to protest the fatal shooting of a woman by a federal immigration officer there and the shooting of two protesters in Portland, Oregon, as Minnesota leaders urged demonstrators to remain peaceful. (AP video: Mike Householder)


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Venezuelans in Madrid call for the release of political prisoners in Venezuela
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Venezuelans living in Madrid gathered Saturday at a public square in the Spanish capital to demand the release of all political prisoners held in Venezuela. It follows a decision by the Venezuelan government on Thursday to release a number of imprisoned high-profile opposition figures, activists and journalists. (AP video shot by Alicia León Perea).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Hundreds in North Carolina protest after ICE shooting in Minneapolis
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of people marched through Durham, North Carolina Saturday as part of nationwide protests against the Trump Administration.

items.title: Families wait for rescuers to retrieve bodies after Philippines landfill avalanche
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>An avalanche of garbage and debris buried and killed at least four workers and left more than 30 others missing in the village of Binaliw in Cebu City, authorities said. (AP video/Jacqueline Hernandez).

#philippines

items.title: Video shows protesters in the streets of Tehran during Iran blackout
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Protests sweeping across Iran neared the two-week mark Saturday, with the country’s government acknowledging the ongoing demonstrations despite an intensifying crackdown and as the Islamic Republic remains cut off from the rest of the world.

#iran #tehran

items.title: How to start strength training after menopause
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Medical experts say strength training keeps bones and muscles healthy after menopause when estrogen loss speeds up a reduction in bone density and contributes to the gradual loss of muscle mass. Here's how to get started at home.</article>

items.title: Hundreds in Syria flee Aleppo neighborhood as forces move into the area
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Syrian security forces began deploying Saturday in a neighborhood in the northern city of Aleppo amid sporadic gunfire after days of intense clashes between government forces and Kurdish fighters that killed and wounded dozens. (AP video/Ghaith Alsayyed).

#syria 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thousands rally with Uganda opposition leader Bobi Wine ahead of election
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Bobi Wine, a musician-turned-politician whose real name is Kyagulanyi Ssentamu, wore a flak jacket and helmet as he waved at supporters from the top of a vehicle on his way to the rally in Mukono. His supporters rallied alongside him, some riding vehicles, others running on foot, as he made his way through the streets. (AP video by Patrick Onen).

#uganda 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Video shows masked and armed men beating a Palestinian man in the West Bank
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Video filmed by security cameras on Thursday shows men dressed mostly in black, and with their faces covered, with several hitting and kicking a man on the ground. Two people who witnessed the attack said 67-year-old Basim Saleh Yassin was beaten by the attackers, believed to be Israeli settlers, as he was trying to flee the nursery in the northern West Bank village of Deir Sharaf. 

#westbank 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Firefighters battle flames as wildfires spread in El Hoyo, Argentina
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Since Monday, flames have spread across a vast area of the "Comarca Andina" in southern Argentina, considered one of the top tourist destinations in the heart of the Andes Mountains. More than 350 people were attempting to halt the fire with the help of air support — including helicopters, amphibious planes, and air tankers — according to the government of Chubut province. (AP video by Maxi Jonas).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Faith leaders demand justice and protesters demonstrate after Minneapolis ICE shooting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The leaders honored Renee Good's memory amidst the Trump Administration's latest immigration crackdown in major U.S. cities, and protesters gathered in Minneapolis to demonstrate against U.S. immigration enforcement.

items.title: Triplets pursue dream of representing Jamaica in ski racing at Winter Olympics
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The teenage ski-racing triplets Helaina, Henri IV, and Henniyah Rivers are on a mission to represent their mother's country of Jamaica at the Milan Cortina Winter Games. On top of that, open more doors for Black athletes in a predominantly white sport. (AP video by Brittany Peterson). Read more: https://bit.ly/45DclBJ

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: New video shows shooting of Renee Good from perspective of ICE officer
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>The video, published by Alpha News and re-posted on social media by the Department of Homeland Security, shows the perspective of ICE officer Jonathan Ross, who fired the shots. It shows Renee Good and Good's wife in a series of exchanges with officers.</article>

<article>Shabana Azeez teases development of her character Victoria in the second season of “The Pitt."
